findings.evidence: Grep shows the new `def primeDiscriminantRadicand` at line 102 and only case lemmas afterward; there is no `primeDiscriminantRadicand_def` theorem.
findings.fix: Add `theorem primeDiscriminantRadicand_def (D : ℤ) : primeDiscriminantRadicand D = if D = -4 ∨ D = 8 ∨ D = -8 then evenPrimeDiscriminantRadicand D else D := rfl`.
findings.issue: `primeDiscriminantRadicand` has no `primeDiscriminantRadicand_def` restatement, so consumers must unfold the public definition to see the even/odd split.

findings.evidence: Line 120 declares `theorem primeDiscriminantRadicand_of_isEvenPrimeDiscriminant`; line 127 marks the analogous odd case `@[simp] theorem primeDiscriminantRadicand_oddPrimeDiscriminant`.
findings.fix: Mark this theorem `@[simp]` if it is intended as the characteristic even-case rewrite.
findings.issue: The even-case normal form `primeDiscriminantRadicand_of_isEvenPrimeDiscriminant` is not marked `@[simp]`, while the parallel odd case is, so `simp` cannot normalize `primeDiscriminantRadicand D` from an `IsEvenPrimeDiscriminant D` hypothesis.

summary: The package exposes the intended predicate and radicand map, but the radicand definition is missing the characteristic public rewrite API expected for downstream use without unfolding.
